name: Lilyana Guinto
ai_summary: Lilyana Guinto is a 19-year-old psychology major at University of Arizona with summer camp counseling experience working with ages 6-12. She brings enthusiasm and positivity to create safe spaces where children feel comfortable, and uses reflective conflict resolution to help students understand root causes.

transcripts.q3: Hi. My name is Liliana ginto I'm 19. I am currently in my second year at the University of Arizona I'm majoring in Psychology and minoring in educational psychology. I've been working with kids since last year officially, I got a job as a summer camp, counselor with the Girl Scouts of Southern Arizona. And from there, I gained a program, facilitator position over there, where I go into different schools. Schools and I helped out in placing a curriculum with the kids. I work from ages around 6 all the way up till 12 primarily. I do somewhat work with younger kids. As I do help coach app coach with baseball and softball for my little siblings. So I stay involved that way with the younger kids. I love working with kids. I've always loved working with kids. I've always had like a knack for it. Almost I would help out a lot growing up with my mom's work. They had a summer camp every year and I always volunteered to be like us Co count camp counselor like a junior camp counselor for them and I just fell in love with it that way. It's part of what I want to do in the future, I do hope to become a form of a child counselor with the degree I'm currently Learning and yeah, I love working with kids because I love how their imagination works and how they just have that genuine excitement and joy for such little things that I feel like people don't appreciate as much now in days.
transcripts.q4: I'm currently located in Tucson Arizona. My house is more on the east side of Tucson, but I'm willing to travel anywhere in Tucson. So, mile radius wise I'd say about from 15 to 20 miles.
transcripts.q5: I would prefer a part-time position just because my schedule right now with school and work is a little chaotic and I would like to find ways to fit this position into it and in between my schedule which is why I had shown interest in the first place because it did to talk about a flexible schedule. But yeah, I mainly prefer part-time just because I am a full-time student.
transcripts.q6: So when it comes to conflict inside of a classroom, my first priority is to separate them just because I don't want to the back and forth, I like to check on the person, that's more distraught. So in this situation, the person that likely got hit I like to pull them aside and talk to them at. But I also like getting both perspectives of the situation just to gain like a deeper understanding of what happened and when it comes to like the actions, maybe reflect with them on it. Such as, for example, the person that hit Them being like, do you think it's okay for us to express our anger through physical contact or using phrases such as well. Would you like it if they hate you, things like that. Just kind of hoping to help them reflect on their actions and understand why that behavior isn't okay. And truly find. The reason why that behavior was occurring in the first place. So if they're having conflicts on and off just because Of something small then maybe trying to find a resolution with both of them but definitely pulling them aside getting both perspectives before trying to find a solution that can help fit for both.
transcripts.q7: I believe I would make a good up, kid teacher, just because of the fact that I do show a lot of enthusiasm and positivity. When I do show up around children, it's almost like a completely different version of myself that I present and I bring in, I try my hardest to make sure that they're comfortable, especially since this position requires for you to come in to maybe a place that the kids don't know you and they're used to the regular teacher. And it might cause like some anxiety or some stress. I think I would do a good job at just kind of presenting myself in the form of like, hey like I understand that this changes a little bit scary but I'm here to support you and help you after all and really just trying to be that support system for them. Even if it is just for a day or its temporary just wanting to make sure that the kids know that they're in a safe space that they're able to express whatever they want to express that they're able to be themselves and things like that.
